findings.summary: Component counts, power rails, and BOM entries are accurate; total_components (17) includes G3 which has in_bom=false; USB_DP reported as lacking ESD protection despite D1 (DT1042-04SO) protecting ...

correct.description: The Connector schematic correctly identifies 17 components (16 in-BOM + G3 which is in_bom=false), 4 power rails (3.3V, GND, V_BATT, V_USB), 31 nets, 2 fuses, 2 resistors, 8 connectors, 1 diode, 1 jumper, 3 other. BOM correctly has 12 unique parts, excluding G3 (in_bom=false).
F1 (6V/0.5A/1A on V_BATT) and F3 (6V/2.0A/4.0A on V_USB) are correctly identified as fuse-type protection devices. Their clamp nets are correctly assigned: F3 clamp=V_USB, F1 clamp=V_BATT.

The USB_DP/USB_DM differential pair is correctly identified as type=USB with D1, J3, J5 as shared components. The 5.1k CC pull-down resistors (R16, R17) are correctly classified as resistors and not confused with dividers or filters.
All four UART-named nets are detected in bus_analysis.uart. The lack of devices: [] for each is appropriate since this schematic only routes the signals through connectors without a UART IC present.

incorrect.description: G3 (OSHW_Logo, SparkFun-Aesthetic:Creative_Commons_License footprint) has in_bom=no and on_board=yes in the KiCad source. The analyzer includes it in the components list and counts it in total_components (17) even though it is a board-only graphic element. The BOM and missing_mpn list correctly exclude G3. This inflates total_components by 1 versus the actual BOM-populated component count of 16.

The design_observations reports USB_DM has has_esd_protection=true but USB_DP has has_esd_protection=false. D1 (DT1042-04SO) is a 4-data-line ESD IC that explicitly connects to both USB_DM (pin 4) and USB_DP (pin 6), as confirmed by the protection_devices entry which lists protected_nets: [USB_DM, USB_DP, ...]. The differential_pairs analysis also reports has_esd=false for the USB pair even though D1 appears in shared_ics. Both USB_DP and the USB differential pair should report ESD protection as true.

missed.description: The Connector board routes I2C signals ESP_22/SCL and ESP_21/SDA through J4 (Qwiic 4-pin JST 1mm connector). The bus_analysis.i2c list is empty. Since the signals traverse only connectors with no driving IC on this sub-board, the detector does not recognize the I2C bus. This is a structural limitation but still a missed observation for the net classification which correctly identifies ESP_22/SCL as a clock net.

findings.summary: Component counts, capacitor counts, and power rail detection are correct; total_components (27) includes G2 which has in_bom=false; RC filter correctly detected: R2 (10k) + C7 (1.0uF) forming RESET...

correct.description: Display schematic correctly identifies 27 total components (26 in-BOM + G2 which is in_bom=false), 3 power rails (3.3V, GND, VUSB), 38 nets, 8 capacitors, 5 resistors, 4 connectors, 4 fiducials, 1 IC, 1 LED, 1 switch, 1 jumper, 2 other.

The RC low-pass filter formed by R2 (10k) and C7 (1.0uF) connected to the RESET net of U1 (SSD1306 OLED) is correctly detected. Cutoff frequency of 15.92 Hz (1/(2π×10kΩ×1μF)) is correct. Input net is 3.3V, output net is RESET, ground net is GND.

The OLED U1 is correctly identified as an I2C device with connections on ESP_21/SDA and ESP_22/SCL. The has_pullup=false flag is correct since no pull-up resistors appear in this sub-board schematic (they reside on the main board).

The decoupling_analysis correctly identifies C4 (1.0uF), C6 (1.0uF), and C16 (0.1uF) as decoupling capacitors for the 3.3V rail, totaling 2.1uF. has_bulk=true, has_bypass=true, has_high_freq=false is accurate. C1 (2.2uF) is correctly omitted as it connects to U1's VCC pin separately.

incorrect.description: G2 (OSHW_Logo) in the Display schematic has in_bom=no and on_board=yes. The analyzer includes it in components and counts it in total_components=27 although it is a board-only graphic with no electrical function. The true BOM component count is 26. This is the same issue as the Connector schematic with G3.

missed.description: The microSD connector J2 is connected via SPI signals SD_COPI, SD_CIPO, SD_SCK, and SD_~{CS} (global labels). These constitute a clear SPI interface. The bus_analysis.spi list is empty and no memory_interfaces are detected. The absence is because no SPI controller IC is present in this sub-schematic—the signals enter via global labels from the main board. While structurally understandable, the SPI bus topology is unobserved.

findings.summary: Footprint count, board dimensions, net count, and routing completeness are accurate; JP0 footprint type stored as 'allow_soldermask_bridges' instead of a valid mount type; SMD and THT counts are co...

correct.description: 38 footprints match the 38 found in the source PCB. Board dimensions 53.0 × 23.0mm are correct. Net count of 31 matches the schematic. The board is fully routed (routing_complete=true, unrouted_net_count=0) with 132 track segments and 29 vias. GND zone stitching correctly detected on both copper layers.

SMD=10 (R16, R17, D1, F1, F3, J1, J2, J3, J4, J9) and THT=3 (J11, J12, J5/USB-C) are correct based on attr flags in source. J5 (USB-C) is classified THT because its shell/mechanical pins are thru_hole; the signal pads are SMD. The 25 remaining footprints are logos, graphics, and panel elements excluded from the smd/tht counts.

incorrect.description: JP0 (Jumper_2_PTH_SMD_Combo-NC) has KiCad attribute (attr allow_soldermask_bridges). The analyzer takes the first attr flag as the footprint type, resulting in type='allow_soldermask_bridges'. This is not a valid mount category—the footprint should be classified as 'smd' or 'through_hole' based on its pads (it has both SMD and THT pads). The allow_soldermask_bridges flag is a DFM setting unrelated to mount type. This also means JP0 is not counted in smd_count (10) or tht_count (3).

suggestions: Fix: JP0 footprint type stored as 'allow_soldermask_bridges' instead of a valid mount type

findings.summary: Footprint count, board dimensions, net count, and unrouted status are accurate; SMD and THT footprint counts are correct for the Display board; DFM metrics missing track/drill data for unrouted boa...

correct.description: 39 footprints match the source. Board dimensions 49.5 × 22.0mm are correct. Net count of 38 matches the schematic. The board is correctly identified as unrouted (track_segments=0, via_count=0, routing_complete=false, unrouted_net_count=20). The 20 unrouted nets are accurately enumerated.

SMD=24 and THT=1 (J3, 1x04 PTH I2C connector) are accurate. J3 has no attr node in the source but is correctly inferred as through_hole from its pad types. The 13 board_only and 1 exclude_from_bom footprints are correctly not counted in smd/tht.

The Display PCB is fully unrouted so DFM metrics only report board dimensions without min_track_width_mm or min_drill_mm. The Connector PCB (which is routed) correctly reports these values. The omission for the unrouted board is appropriate.

findings.summary: Display non-production gerbers: layer set complete, board dimensions match PCB, alignment verified

correct.description: The Display gerber set (non-panelized, from Hardware/Display/) correctly finds all 9 required layers, reports board dimensions of 49.5 × 22.0mm (matching the PCB), alignment=true. Drill classification correctly identifies 54 vias and 8 component holes. The routed gerbers coexist with the unrouted PCB file (the gerbers are from a previously manufactured revision).

findings.summary: Alignment flagged as failed for panel gerbers — expected behavior for panelization

incorrect.description: Hardware_Connector_Production contains panel gerbers (files named *-Panel.*) with board dimensions 120.22 × 203.67mm vs. individual copper extents of ~112 × 179mm. The alignment check reports width/height variation of ~8.5mm and ~24.6mm due to panel rails that extend the edge cuts beyond the active copper area. This is normal for panelized production files and should not be flagged as an alignment defect. The same issue applies to Hardware_Display_Production, Hardware_Main_Production, and Hardware_Main-LBand_Production.

findings.summary: layer_count=2 is wrong; Main Production panel is a 4-layer board with GL2 and GL3 inner copper files

incorrect.description: The Hardware/Main/Production/ directory contains SparkFun RTK Facet - Main-Panel.GL2 and .GL3 files which are RS-274X EAGLE gerber exports for inner copper layers In1.Cu and In2.Cu. The analyzer's file discovery uses glob patterns for .G2/.G3 (Protel style) and .G2L/.G3L but does not include .GL2/.GL3 (a different Protel variant where the L precedes the digit). As a result, GL2 and GL3 are not discovered, not parsed, and not counted. layer_count reports 2 instead of the correct 4.

findings.summary: layer_count=2 is wrong; Main-LBand Production panel is a 4-layer board with GL2 and GL3 files

incorrect.description: The Hardware/Main-LBand/Production/ directory contains SparkFun RTK Facet L-Band - Main-Panel.GL2 (1.2MB) and .GL3 (1.9MB) EAGLE RS-274X gerber files for inner copper layers. These are not recognized by the analyzer because .GL2/.GL3 extensions are absent from both the glob pattern list and the Protel inner layer extension set. The GL2 and GL3 files are genuine inner copper layers with substantial content (per file sizes), confirming a 4-layer board. The analyzer reports layer_count=2 and completeness.found_layers lists only F.Cu and B.Cu.

findings.summary: Retension plate: layer count, hole classification, and layer completeness are correct; Alignment flagged as failed for Retension plate due to minimal copper extent vs large board outline

correct.description: The retention plate gerbers correctly identify 2 copper layers, 4 mounting holes (2.3mm diameter) with zero vias and zero component holes. All 9 standard layers are present. The board is a mechanical retention fixture with very little copper content which is accurate.

incorrect.description: The Retension plate (106 × 58mm) has very small copper features: B.Cu extends only 10.66 × 0.797mm and F.Cu only 8.154 × 2.805mm, because this is a pure mechanical retention plate with minimal copper. The alignment checker reports width variation of 97.8mm and height variation of 57.2mm. While technically correct (copper doesn't fill the board), this is expected and intentional for a retention plate and should not be treated as a fabrication defect.

findings.summary: Display Production panel gerbers: layer set complete, drill files present and correctly classified

correct.description: All 9 standard layers found, PTH drill file present, no missing required layers. Total 1008 holes and 7596 flashes reflect a fully populated panel. The 2-layer designation is correct (no inner copper files exist in Hardware/Display/Production/).
